summary: DHH celebrates converting a developer named Marten from the .NET ecosystem to Ruby on Rails, Mac, and TextMate. Marten had previously been involved in a Rails.NET project but has since switched to the full Ruby on Rails stack. DHH frames this with playful self-awareness as his 'Jedi mind tricks' at work, welcoming Marten to what he calls 'a better world.'
key_points: A .NET developer who had tried to port Rails concepts to .NET was convinced to switch to Ruby on Rails instead
The conversion extended beyond just the framework to the full ecosystem: Ruby, Mac, and TextMate
DHH frames platform evangelism as a quasi-religious conversion experience with humor and confidence
money_quotes: So it seems that my Jedi mind tricks are quite operational.
Welcome to a better world, Marten. You're going to love it here.
key_insight: DHH saw Rails adoption not as a mere technical choice but as a full ecosystem conversion — framework, language, OS, and editor — and evangelized it with unapologetic confidence.
